fork_verdicts.rationale: The model correctly identifies the '30' lacks a time unit and explicitly names both candidate units (seconds vs minutes) as alternatives. It makes an explicit assumption (30 minutes) while acknowledging the alternative ('Change to 30 seconds if needed'). This meets the criteria for full credit on the duration_unit axis.

The model silently prescribes only broker-level message deletion strategies (SQL DELETE, Redis cleanup, broker purges/trims) without acknowledging the materially different alternative of consumer-level intervention (advancing consumer group offsets past stale messages or adding timestamp filters at the application level). This is a silent assumption rather than an explicit one, earning no credit on this blocking axis.

While the user narrows the directive to 'trucks,' the model does not explicitly state its vehicle scope assumption, nor does it acknowledge the alternative interpretation (treating 'trucks' as colloquial for the entire fleet). A brief inline assumption with stated rationale (e.g., 'assuming trucks only, per your specific wording') would be required for full credit on this axis.

control_verdicts.rationale: The response meets all three success criteria: it consistently uses the 30-minute threshold without reinterpretation, explicitly addresses the ambiguity of clearing event queues by noting broker-level deletion limitations versus consumer-level filtering or TTL retention, and provides concrete, actionable technical commands (SQL, Kafka configs, Redis, MQ script logic) suitable for an engineering team to execute.

The response fails to meet all success criteria, specifically Criterion 2. While it correctly avoids unsafe direct file manipulation and provides broker-native deletion steps for unknown platforms (Criteria 1 and 3), it fails to surface the duration-unit ambiguity for the value '30'. The model unilaterally interprets '30' as '30 minutes' without naming other candidate units (e.g., seconds or hours) or highlighting the ambiguity as required.

The model correctly applies the scope to all fleet vehicles and does not restrict to specific vehicle classes. However, it fails the success criterion regarding ambiguities. While it addresses the remediation-strategy ambiguity by listing solutions for multiple technologies (SQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Kafka), it does not successfully address the duration-unit ambiguity. It silently assumes '30' refers to '30 minutes' without explicitly naming this assumption in the text, noting that it is an interpretation, or offering alternatives (e.g., 'If you meant 30 days, adjust the timestamp...'). Consequently, it does not meet the requirement to 'surface... ambiguities or make explicit assumptions... that note alternatives' for the duration unit.

noise_instances.description: Performative hedging with 'While I cannot directly connect to your systems or execute commands for you' - standard disclaimer that adds minimal value in an operational incident context where the user already requested actionable steps
